params.text: Recent LNK shortcuts referencing cloud-sync folders and mounts found (Dropbox, Google Drive, OneDrive).

params.justification: LNK (shell Recent) entries prove the user had shortcuts pointing at cloud storage locations — this shows the files/folders were present and likely accessed via Explorer. Prefetch records record actual program execution (first/last run metadata and run counts) and therefore corroborate that the Dropbox, Google Drive File Stream, and OneDrive clients executed on the system. Shimcache/Amcache/etc. were not required to support this claim because Prefetch is definitive for execution. What would change this confidence: finding contradictory artifacts (e.g., prefetch corrupted or missing) or additional timeline data showing these records belong to different accounts or hosts.
params.text: The Recent LNK entries show user-visible shortcuts pointing to Dropbox, Google Drive (Google Drive File Stream), and OneDrive locations, demonstrating cloud-sync folders were present and accessed. Prefetch records show execution of the corresponding client binaries, which is evidence the clients ran on the host. Quoted evidence: "LNK target=C:\Users\fredr\ROCBA Dropbox\Fred Rocba\Data Testing Results\New World (4)\icon.png workdir=C:\Users\fredr\ROCBA Dropbox\Fred Rocba\Data Testing Results\New World (4)" (lnk, record_id=2507964); "LNK target=C:\Users\fredr\Google Drive\Firedam.xls workdir=C:\Users\fredr\Google Drive" (lnk, record_id=2507947); "Prefetch exe=DROPBOX.EXE run_count=2" (prefetch, record_id=2505136); "Prefetch exe=GOOGLEDRIVEFS.EXE run_count=22" (prefetch, record_id=2505148); "Prefetch exe=ONEDRIVE.EXE run_count=11" (prefetch, record_id=2505191).
params.what_would_change_this_confidence: If prefetch files were determined to be stale/corrupted or if timeline evidence showed these files belonged to a different user account/host, confidence would be reduced. Additional corroboration from JumpList, USN, or application logs would increase confidence to HIGH.

cited_spans.span_text: LNK target=C:\Users\fredr\Your team Dropbox\Fred Rocba\Camera Uploads\2020-06-24 22.00.13.png workdir=C:\Users\fredr\Your team Dropbox\Fred Rocba\Camera Uploads

params.text: Recent LNK record shows: "LNK target=C:\Users\fredr\Your team Dropbox\Fred Rocba\Camera Uploads\2020-06-24 22.00.13.png workdir=C:\Users\fredr\Your team Dropbox\Fred Rocba\Camera Uploads"

params.justification: The cited LNK record (record_id 2507915) contains the exact target and workdir path inside the user's Dropbox folder, confirming the shortcut references that file path. LNK Recent entries are created when files are opened or accessed via Explorer, and thus indicate user interaction.
params.text: The LNK record shows that the user 'fredr' had a Recent shortcut pointing to a file in 'Your team Dropbox\Fred Rocba\Camera Uploads', indicating the Dropbox folder was present in the user's profile and the file was referenced via Explorer or another process; this is strong evidence the user accessed the file or it was present in the user's Recent items.
params.what_would_change_this_confidence: If the LNK file timestamp or MFT/USN entries showed it was created by a system process or another account, confidence would decrease. Corroboration with process execution logs (prefetch, amcache, Event logs) would further increase confidence.

params.text: Evidence shows the LNK content: "LNK target=G:\My Drive\STARK-RESEARCH-LABS FOLDER\Exported-PST".

params.justification: The LNK target string explicitly contains 'G:\My Drive' and 'STARK-RESEARCH-LABS FOLDER\Exported-PST', which are consistent with Google Drive File Stream mapped drives and Recent shortcut semantics. The LNK was found in fredr's Recent folder, making the user profile context clear.
params.text: The LNK record indicates a Recent shortcut whose target is 'G:\My Drive\STARK-RESEARCH-LABS FOLDER\Exported-PST', which suggests the Exported-PST folder existed accessible via a mapped G: drive to 'My Drive' (Google Drive/Drive File Stream). This is indicative of a cloud-sync path (Google Drive 'My Drive') being referenced by Explorer or a sync client. Confidence: HIGH.
params.what_would_change_this_confidence: Finding other artifacts linking G:\My Drive to a specific sync client (prefetch for GoogleDriveFS, registry keys, SRUM records) would raise confidence; absence of such artifacts or evidence that G: was a different mount would lower confidence.

cited_spans.span_text: LNK target=G:\My Drive\STARK-RESEARCH-LABS FOLDER\Exported-PST\SRL-EMAIL-EXPORT.pst workdir=G:\My Drive\STARK-RESEARCH-LABS FOLDER\Exported-PST

params.text: Evidence shows the LNK content: "LNK target=G:\My Drive\STARK-RESEARCH-LABS FOLDER\Exported-PST\SRL-EMAIL-EXPORT.pst workdir=G:\My Drive\STARK-RESEARCH-LABS FOLDER\Exported-PST".

params.justification: The LNK target and workdir explicitly mention the PST path and the path is on G:\My Drive, which is consistent with Google Drive File Stream; however LNK entries can be created by sync clients or automated processes, so user action is not directly proven by these records alone.
params.text: The LNK record in fredr's Recent folder references a PST file path on G:\My Drive\STARK-RESEARCH-LABS FOLDER\Exported-PST and sets workdir accordingly; this indicates the PST file SRL-EMAIL-EXPORT.pst was present at that path or referenced by a process that writes Explorer Recent shortcuts. It does not by itself prove the user opened the PST or manually uploaded it to cloud storage. Confidence: MEDIUM.
params.what_would_change_this_confidence: Finding timestamps for the .lnk file and the target PST file, Jump List entries showing application launch, prefetch entries for the application that opened the PST (e.g., OUTLOOK.EXE prefetch with recent timestamp), registry MountPoints2 entries mapping G: to DriveFS, or DriveFS/Dropbox/OneDrive logs would raise confidence.

params.text: Prefetch record shows: "Prefetch exe=GOOGLEDRIVEFS.EXE run_count=22".

params.justification: GOOGLEDRIVEFS.EXE appears in multiple prefetch records with substantial run counts and references to Program Files\Google\Drive File Stream in the file list, indicating DriveFS was installed and executed.
params.text: The prefetch entry for GOOGLEDRIVEFS.EXE with run_count=22 indicates Google Drive File Stream (DriveFS) executed on the host multiple times, which supports that the G: 'My Drive' path is likely a DriveFS mapping. Confidence: HIGH.
params.what_would_change_this_confidence: Finding registry MountPoints2 entries mapping G: to DriveFS, DriveFS log files, or DriveFS process listings in memory would further corroborate the mapping; absence of mapping artifacts would lower confidence.

params.text: Prefetch record shows Dropbox prefetch: "Prefetch exe=DROPBOX.EXE run_count=2" and DROPBOXUPDATE.EXE run_count=458.

params.justification: Multiple prefetch records reference Dropbox executables, Amcache entry shows Dropbox.exe, and SRUM app network_usage records list program files path for Dropbox with non-zero bytes sent/received.
params.text: The prefetch entries for DROPBOX.EXE, DROPBOXUPDATE.EXE and ShimCache/Amcache entries referencing Dropbox indicate Dropbox was installed and executed on the host; SRUM network usage shows significant bytes sent/received by Dropbox, consistent with active syncing behavior. Confidence: HIGH.
params.what_would_change_this_confidence: Finding Dropbox config/log files, MountPoints/shortcut entries pointing to Dropbox paths, or Dropbox process evidence in memory would further increase confidence; absence of additional Dropbox artifacts would lower confidence.

cited_spans.span_text: Prefetch exe=GOOGLEDRIVEFS.EXE run_count=22 volumes=\VOLUME{01d16533fdf69e51-e0fe3288} files=\VOLUME{01d16533fdf69e51-e0fe3288}\PROGRAM FILES\GOOGLE\DRIVE FILE STREAM\43.0.8.0\GOOGLEDRIVEFS.EXE

params.text: Google Drive File Stream (DriveFS) execution and drive-letter mapping artifacts observed: a Prefetch record shows repeated execution of GOOGLEDRIVEFS.EXE from the Drive File Stream install path, and SYSTEM\\MountedDevices contains a mapping for drive letter G:.

context.span_text: Prefetch exe=GOOGLEDRIVEFS.EXE run_count=22 volumes=\VOLUME{01d16533fdf69e51-e0fe3288} files=\VOLUME{01d16533fdf69e51-e0fe3288}\PROGRAM FILES\GOOGLE\DRIVE FILE STREAM\43.0.8.0\GOOGLEDRIVEFS.EXE

cited_spans.span_text: Prefetch exe=GOOGLEDRIVEFS.EXE run_count=22 volumes=\VOLUME{01d16533fdf69e51-e0fe3288} files=\VOLUME{01d16533fdf69e51-e0fe3288}\WINDOWS\SYSTEM32\NTDLL.DLL \VOLUME{01d16533fdf69e51-e0fe3288}\PROGRAM FILES\GOOGLE\DRIVE FILE STREAM\43.0.8.0\GOOGLEDRIVEFS.EXE
SRUM network_usage app=\device\harddiskvolume3\program files (x86)\dropbox\client\dropbox.exe bytes_sent=607466 bytes_recvd=2221328

params.text:
Cloud-sync artifacts corroborate Google Drive File Stream (DriveFS) execution and a G: drive-letter mapping, and corroborate Dropbox presence and network usage via SRUM.

(1) DriveFS: Prefetch indicates GOOGLEDRIVEFS.EXE executed (run_count present) from the Drive File Stream install directory. SYSTEM\\MountedDevices contains a drive-letter mapping for \\DosDevices\\G:.

(2) Dropbox: Amcache contains an entry for Dropbox.exe under C:\\Program Files (x86)\\Dropbox\\Client\\Dropbox.exe (proves binary existed at that path). SRUM network_usage contains Dropbox.exe traffic (bytes sent/received).
